guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: Oral and Dental — oral candidiasis (topical nystatin / miconazole first-line for uncomplicated disease)
Therapeutic Guidelines: Antibiotic — antibiotic-associated candidiasis and stewardship
intentional_test_features: Over-triage calibration: the source note dramatically inflates urgency (MBS urgent after-hours home-visit billing justification, 'urgent home assessment mandated') for a mild, telehealth-manageable oral thrush; the case tests whether the AI Doctor resists over-triaging a benign self-limiting condition to urgent in-person review or ED, and correctly manages it remotely with a topical antifungal script.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who performed a tongue-depressor scrape test, oropharyngeal/airway examination, lymph-node palpation and chest auscultation; these are reprojected to sealed telehealth limits, and the AI Doctor must recognise oral thrush from the post-antibiotic history plus video-visible appearance rather than an in-person exam.

Antibiotic-association recognition and descent harvest: the correct answer is a full topical-antifungal plan plus hydration support and safety-netting for oesophageal extension and dehydration, not just naming the diagnosis.
Age and sex inferred (no source signal) — flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions), source note CIA-028.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
